title: 命案目睹记
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\136-命案目睹记-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Alfred proposes a partnership to Lucy involving the illegal sale of gold blocks through legal maneuvering. He suggests marriage as a condition, noting that a wife cannot testify against her husband, and mentions he will soon inherit money from his "stingy, old and cruel" father. Lucy feels a primal attraction to him but declines to play games, focusing on dinner.

After dinner, Harold meets Lucy in the parlor. He expresses appreciation for her abilities, stating her talents are being wasted, and offers her a high-paying position with a bright future in London if she contacts his secretary. Lucy considers the offer. Later, Lucy encounters Cecil on the stairs; after a brief misunderstanding regarding marriage, Cecil tells her she is the last woman he would want to marry.

In a separate location, Clodock meets with Amadeus Deschamps, the head of the Paris Police Department, to discuss a hypothesis regarding a strangled woman. They seek information about Anna Stravinsky, a dancer from the Mariinsky Ballet. They visit Madame Jeannot (also referred to as Madame Jolet), who runs the troupe. Madame Jeannot describes Anna as an average dancer who used a pseudonym. She notes that Anna claimed to have an English husband and disappeared around the Sunday before Christmas, shortly after the troupe's tour of various English locations including Hammersmith and Torquay. When Clodock reveals they believe Anna was murdered, Madame Jeannot mentions that Anna was a devout Catholic who attended Sunday Mass to confess.
